channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia monitors Ebola outbreak but maintains current border measures
item.description: Australia is keeping a close eye on the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, which has now surpassed 1,000 suspected cases. While countries like the United States a...

item.title: Saturday Fiddle Golf Tournament Promises Sports, Food, and Entertainment in Dar es Salaam
item.description: Dar es Salaam golfers and social lovers are in for a special treat as the 'Saturday Fiddle' golf event is set for June 6, 2026, at the TPDF Lugalo Golf Club. The tournament blends competitive 18-hole ...

item.title: Pam Bondi recovering after thyroid cancer surgery while remaining active in Trump administration advisory roles
item.description: Former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi is recovering after undergoing surgery for thyroid cancer a few weeks ago, according to comments she made in an interview with CNN. Bondi stated that she is stil...

item.title: Krishnagiri Collector reviews school exam performance and directs remedial measures
item.description: A review meeting was held at the Krishnagiri Collectorate chaired by Collector C. Dinesh Kumar to assess the performance of schools following the recently declared Class X and Class XII board examinat...

item.title: UK advisory committee rejects expanded prostate cancer screening programme amid ongoing debate over early detection
item.description: The UK National Screening Committee has recommended against introducing a broad national prostate cancer screening programme, a decision that has sparked strong criticism from campaigners, charities, ...

item.title: President Marcos Highlights Japan’s Key Contributions to Philippine Economic Growth
item.description: During his state visit to Japan, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. delivered a historic speech before the National Diet, emphasizing Japan's crucial role in the Philippines' economic development since th...

item.title: South Africa’s Fuel Security Under Pressure Amid Iran War and Weak Strategic Oil Reserves
item.description: This article talks about how South Africa’s fuel supply is under serious pressure because of global oil disruptions linked to the conflict involving Israel, the US, and Iran, which has affected oil fl...

item.title: Jayme Fressard scores try against Queensland Maroons
item.description: In a thrilling NRL encounter, Jayme Fressard showcased her agility and speed by slicing through the Queensland Maroons' defensive line to score a remarkable try. Fressard's well-timed run and ability ...

item.title: Several Musicians Withdraw from Freedom 250 Festival Over Political Concerns
item.description: Several high-profile artists have withdrawn from the Freedom 250 festival, an event linked to former President Donald Trump, amid concerns over its political affiliations. The festival, which is being...

item.title: Nigeria’s ransom economy: how insecurity fuels a parallel cost of living
item.description: In this Premium Times opinion by Lanre Ogundipe, the author argues that Nigeria’s insecurity has evolved from a security problem into a macroeconomic crisis. He questions who profits from prolonged in...

item.title: Five suspects return to Pretoria High Court for Senzo Meyiwa murder trial
item.description: Five men accused of killing soccer star Senzo Meyiwa returned to the dock at the Pretoria High Court on Thursday, continuing a trial that has spanned many years since the 2014 murder. The article note...

item.title: Spencer Pratt claims divine support in Los Angeles mayoral race during Fox News interview

item.title: Republican Officials Cautious About Attending Bruce Springsteen Concerts Amid Trump Tensions
item.description: Several Republican operatives and Trump administration staffers who are fans of Bruce Springsteen face a difficult choice as the rock star tours with his 'No Kings Tour.' They fear that attending his ...

item.title: NACTVET launches 2026/27 admissions for certificate and diploma programmes through centralized system
item.description: Tanzania’s National Council for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (NACTVET) has officially opened the application window for certificate and diploma programmes for the 2026/27 academic y...

item.title: Sinner shock French Open defeat highlights heat and pressure issues
item.description: Jannik Sinner’s unexpected and dramatic defeat at the French Open has been described as a major shock that could reshape the remainder of the tournament. The world number one, widely regarded as the d...

item.title: Bank of Canada Highlights Growing Economic and Geopolitical Risks to Financial Stability
item.description: The Bank of Canada’s latest Financial Stability Report indicates that while Canada’s financial system remains resilient, it faces rising risks from a volatile global and domestic environment. Househol...

item.title: JPMorgan Chase open to major acquisitions of up to $20 billion under Jamie Dimon
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon indicated that the bank could pursue significant acquisitions over the next few years, potentially deploying between $10 billion and $20 billion to purchase a suitable t...

item.title: Satirical campaign diary portrays a Reform UK candidate in a fictional political sketch
item.description: This article is a satirical political sketch written in the form of a fictional campaign diary by John Crace. It follows the imagined daily activities and thoughts of Robert Kenyon, a Reform UK candid...

item.title: Robin Padilla pushes for online Senate participation for Bato dela Rosa amid ICC arrest warrant
item.description: Senator Robin Padilla defended Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa and said he should still be allowed to perform his duties as a lawmaker through online participation despite the arrest warrant issued ag...

item.title: Katambi Highlights Discipline, Accountability and Community Role in Tanzania Security Forces
item.description: Tanzania's Minister for Home Affairs, Patrobas Katambi, during a graduation ceremony for new police officers and assistant inspectors at Kurasini in Dar es Salaam, stressed the need for professionalis...
